<python_code>
import numpy as np
from scipy.spatial.transform import Rotation as R

# Step 1: Open gripper and go to home position
open_gripper()
goto_home_joint_position()

# Step 2: Get grasp pose for the extruded handle of the brown square nut
handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz = sample_grasp_pose('extruded handle of the brown square nut')

# Step 3: Approach and grasp the handle
goto_pose(handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz, z_approach=0.1)  # Approach from 10cm above
goto_pose(handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz)  # Move to actual grasp pose
close_gripper()  # Close gripper to grasp the nut

# Step 4: Get the center of the brown square nut to compute relative transform
center_position, center_quaternion_wxyz = get_object_pose('white hollow center of the brown square nut')

# Compute the fixed transform from handle to center (in object frame)
# This transform will be applied in reverse during insertion
delta_pos_handle_to_center = center_position - handle_position
# The orientation difference (from handle grasp frame to nut center frame)
rot_handle = R.from_quat(handle_quaternion_wxyz)  # WXYZ -> XYZW for scipy
rot_center = R.from_quat(center_quaternion_wxyz)
rot_delta_handle_to_center = rot_center * rot_handle.inv()  # Relative rotation

# Step 5: Get the pose of the brown square block (target insertion location)
block_position, block_quaternion_wxyz = get_object_pose('brown square block')

# Compute desired center pose of nut when inserted: aligned with block
inserted_center_position = block_position
inserted_center_quaternion_wxyz = block_quaternion_wxyz

# Now compute the required handle pose given the desired center pose
# Apply inverse of the original delta to get handle pose from center pose
desired_rot = R.from_quat(inserted_center_quaternion_wxyz)
desired_handle_quat = (desired_rot * rot_delta_handle_to_center.inv()).as_quat()  # Convert back to WXYZ
desired_handle_pos = inserted_center_position - (desired_rot.as_matrix() @ rot_handle.as_matrix().T @ delta_pos_handle_to_center)

# Step 6: Insert the nut by moving handle to desired pose
goto_pose(desired_handle_pos, desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz, z_approach=0.1)  # Approach from above
goto_pose(desired_handle_pos, desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz)  # Final insertion pose

# Step 7: Release the nut
open_gripper()

# Step 8: Return to home
goto_home_joint_position()
</python_code>

<python_code>
import numpy as np
from scipy.spatial.transform import Rotation as R

# Step 1: Open gripper and go to home position
open_gripper()
goto_home_joint_position()

# Step 2: Get grasp pose for the extruded handle of the brown square nut
handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz = sample_grasp_pose('extruded handle of the brown square nut')

# Step 3: Approach and grasp the handle
goto_pose(handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz, z_approach=0.1)  # Approach from 10cm above
goto_pose(handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz)  # Move to actual grasp pose
close_gripper()  # Close gripper to grasp the nut

# Step 4: Get the center of the brown square nut to compute relative transform
center_position, center_quaternion_wxyz = get_object_pose('white hollow center of the brown square nut')

# Compute the fixed transform from handle to center (in object frame)
delta_pos_handle_to_center = center_position - handle_position
# The orientation difference (from handle grasp frame to nut center frame)
rot_handle = R.from_quat(handle_quaternion_wxyz[[1,2,3,0]])  # Convert WXYZ to XYZW for scipy
rot_center = R.from_quat(center_quaternion_wxyz[[1,2,3,0]])
rot_delta_handle_to_center = rot_center * rot_handle.inv()  # Relative rotation

# Step 5: Get the pose of the brown square block (target insertion location)
block_position, block_quaternion_wxyz = get_object_pose('brown square block')

# Compute desired center pose of nut when inserted: aligned with block
inserted_center_position = block_position
inserted_center_quaternion_wxyz = block_quaternion_wxyz

# Now compute the required handle pose given the desired center pose
desired_rot = R.from_quat(inserted_center_quaternion_wxyz[[1,2,3,0]])
# Apply inverse of the original delta to get handle pose from center pose
relative_rotation_matrix = (desired_rot * rot_delta_handle_to_center.inv()).as_matrix()
desired_handle_pos = inserted_center_position - relative_rotation_matrix @ delta_pos_handle_to_center
# Convert final quaternion back to WXYZ
final_rot_wxyz = (desired_rot * rot_delta_handle_to_center.inv()).as_quat()  # Returns XYZW
desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz = np.array([final_rot_wxyz[3], final_rot_wxyz[0], final_rot_wxyz[1], final_rot_wxyz[2]])  # Convert to WXYZ

# Step 6: Insert the nut by moving handle to desired pose
goto_pose(desired_handle_pos, desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz, z_approach=0.1)  # Approach from above
goto_pose(desired_handle_pos, desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz)  # Final insertion pose

# Step 7: Release the nut
open_gripper()

# Step 8: Return to home
goto_home_joint_position()
</python_code>

import numpy as np
from scipy.spatial.transform import Rotation as R

# Step 1: Open gripper and go to home position
open_gripper()
goto_home_joint_position()

# Step 2: Get grasp pose for the extruded handle of the brown square nut
handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz = sample_grasp_pose('extruded handle of the brown square nut')

# Step 3: Approach and grasp the handle
goto_pose(handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz, z_approach=0.1)  # Approach from 10cm above
goto_pose(handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz)  # Move to actual grasp pose
close_gripper()  # Close gripper to grasp the nut

# Step 4: Get the center of the brown square nut to compute relative transform
center_position, center_quaternion_wxyz = get_object_pose('white hollow center of the brown square nut')

# Compute the fixed transform from handle to center (in object frame)
delta_pos_handle_to_center = center_position - handle_position
# The orientation difference (from handle grasp frame to nut center frame)
rot_handle = R.from_quat(handle_quaternion_wxyz[[1,2,3,0]])  # Convert WXYZ to XYZW for scipy
rot_center = R.from_quat(center_quaternion_wxyz[[1,2,3,0]])
rot_delta_handle_to_center = rot_center * rot_handle.inv()  # Relative rotation

# Step 5: Get the pose of the brown square block (target insertion location)
block_position, block_quaternion_wxyz = get_object_pose('brown square block')

# Compute desired center pose of nut when inserted: aligned with block
inserted_center_position = block_position
inserted_center_quaternion_wxyz = block_quaternion_wxyz

# Now compute the required handle pose given the desired center pose
desired_rot = R.from_quat(inserted_center_quaternion_wxyz[[1,2,3,0]])
# Apply inverse of the original delta to get handle pose from center pose
relative_rotation_matrix = (desired_rot * rot_delta_handle_to_center.inv()).as_matrix()
desired_handle_pos = inserted_center_position - relative_rotation_matrix @ delta_pos_handle_to_center
# Convert final quaternion back to WXYZ
final_rot_wxyz = (desired_rot * rot_delta_handle_to_center.inv()).as_quat()  # Returns XYZW
desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz = np.array([final_rot_wxyz[3], final_rot_wxyz[0], final_rot_wxyz[1], final_rot_wxyz[2]])  # Convert to WXYZ

# Step 6: Insert the nut by moving handle to desired pose
goto_pose(desired_handle_pos, desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz, z_approach=0.1)  # Approach from above
goto_pose(desired_handle_pos, desired_handle_quaternion_wxyz)  # Final insertion pose

# Step 7: Release the nut
open_gripper()

# Step 8: Return to home
goto_home_joint_position()

trajectory.model_output:
<python_code>
import numpy as np
from scipy.spatial.transform import Rotation as R

# Re-attempt the task with improved reasoning and robustness

# Step 1: Open gripper and go to home position
open_gripper()
goto_home_joint_position()

# Step 2: Get grasp pose for the extruded handle of the brown square nut
handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz = sample_grasp_pose('extruded handle of the brown square nut')

# Step 3: Approach and grasp the handle
goto_pose(handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz, z_approach=0.1)  # Approach from 10cm above
goto_pose(handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz)  # Move to actual grasp pose
close_gripper()  # Close gripper to grasp the nut

# Step 4: Get the center of the brown square nut to compute relative transform
center_position, center_quaternion_wxyz = get_object_pose('white hollow center of the brown square nut')

# Compute the fixed transform (position and orientation) from handle to center
delta_pos_handle_to_center = center_position - handle_position

# Handle rotation (grasp frame) and center rotation (object frame)
rot_handle = R.from_quat(np.roll(handle_quaternion_wxyz, -1))  # WXYZ -> XYZW
rot_center = R.from_quat(np.roll(center_quaternion_wxyz, -1))
# Relative rotation from handle frame to center frame
rot_delta = rot_center * rot_handle.inv()

# Step 5: Get the target block's pose (where nut must be inserted)
block_position, block_quaternion_wxyz = get_object_pose('brown square block')

# Desired final center pose of the nut (aligned with block)
final_center_pos = block_position
final_center_rot = R.from_quat(np.roll(block_quaternion_wxyz, -1))

# Now compute the required handle pose using inverse kinematics:
# Apply the reverse of the original delta to get handle pose from desired center
final_handle_rot = final_center_rot * rot_delta.inv()
final_handle_pos = final_center_pos - final_handle_rot.as_matrix() @ (rot_handle.as_matrix().T @ delta_pos_handle_to_center)

# Convert final handle rotation back to WXYZ
final_handle_quat_wxyz = np.roll(final_handle_rot.as_quat(), 1)  # XYZW -> WXYZ

# Step 6: Insert the nut by moving to the computed handle pose
goto_pose(final_handle_pos, final_handle_quat_wxyz, z_approach=0.1)
goto_pose(final_handle_pos, final_handle_quat_wxyz)

# Step 7: Release the nut
open_gripper()

# Step 8: Return to home
goto_home_joint_position()
</python_code>

<python_code>
import numpy as np
from scipy.spatial.transform import Rotation as R

# Re-attempt the task with improved reasoning and robustness

# Step 1: Open gripper and go to home position
open_gripper()
goto_home_joint_position()

# Step 2: Get grasp pose for the extruded handle of the brown square nut
handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz = sample_grasp_pose('extruded handle of the brown square nut')

# Step 3: Approach and grasp the handle
goto_pose(handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz, z_approach=0.1)  # Approach from 10cm above
goto_pose(handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz)  # Move to actual grasp pose
close_gripper()  # Close gripper to grasp the nut

# Step 4: Get the center of the brown square nut to compute relative transform
center_position, center_quaternion_wxyz = get_object_pose('white hollow center of the brown square nut')

# Compute the fixed transform (position and orientation) from handle to center
delta_pos_handle_to_center = center_position - handle_position

# Handle rotation (grasp frame) and center rotation (object frame)
rot_handle = R.from_quat(np.roll(handle_quaternion_wxyz, -1))  # WXYZ -> XYZW
rot_center = R.from_quat(np.roll(center_quaternion_wxyz, -1))
# Relative rotation from handle frame to center frame
rot_delta = rot_center * rot_handle.inv()

# Step 5: Get the target block's pose (where nut must be inserted)
block_position, block_quaternion_wxyz = get_object_pose('brown square block')

# Desired final center pose of the nut (aligned with block)
final_center_pos = block_position
final_center_rot = R.from_quat(np.roll(block_quaternion_wxyz, -1))

# Now compute the required handle pose using inverse kinematics:
# Apply the reverse of the original delta to get handle pose from desired center
final_handle_rot = final_center_rot * rot_delta.inv()
final_handle_pos = final_center_pos - final_handle_rot.as_matrix() @ (rot_handle.as_matrix().T @ delta_pos_handle_to_center)

# Convert final handle rotation back to WXYZ
final_handle_quat_wxyz = np.roll(final_handle_rot.as_quat(), 1)  # XYZW -> WXYZ

# Step 6: Insert the nut by moving to the computed handle pose
goto_pose(final_handle_pos, final_handle_quat_wxyz, z_approach=0.1)
goto_pose(final_handle_pos, final_handle_quat_wxyz)

# Step 7: Release the nut
open_gripper()

# Step 8: Return to home
goto_home_joint_position()
</python_code>

tool_infos.tool_input:
import numpy as np
from scipy.spatial.transform import Rotation as R

# Re-attempt the task with improved reasoning and robustness

# Step 1: Open gripper and go to home position
open_gripper()
goto_home_joint_position()

# Step 2: Get grasp pose for the extruded handle of the brown square nut
handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz = sample_grasp_pose('extruded handle of the brown square nut')

# Step 3: Approach and grasp the handle
goto_pose(handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz, z_approach=0.1)  # Approach from 10cm above
goto_pose(handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz)  # Move to actual grasp pose
close_gripper()  # Close gripper to grasp the nut

# Step 4: Get the center of the brown square nut to compute relative transform
center_position, center_quaternion_wxyz = get_object_pose('white hollow center of the brown square nut')

# Compute the fixed transform (position and orientation) from handle to center
delta_pos_handle_to_center = center_position - handle_position

# Handle rotation (grasp frame) and center rotation (object frame)
rot_handle = R.from_quat(np.roll(handle_quaternion_wxyz, -1))  # WXYZ -> XYZW
rot_center = R.from_quat(np.roll(center_quaternion_wxyz, -1))
# Relative rotation from handle frame to center frame
rot_delta = rot_center * rot_handle.inv()

# Step 5: Get the target block's pose (where nut must be inserted)
block_position, block_quaternion_wxyz = get_object_pose('brown square block')

# Desired final center pose of the nut (aligned with block)
final_center_pos = block_position
final_center_rot = R.from_quat(np.roll(block_quaternion_wxyz, -1))

# Now compute the required handle pose using inverse kinematics:
# Apply the reverse of the original delta to get handle pose from desired center
final_handle_rot = final_center_rot * rot_delta.inv()
final_handle_pos = final_center_pos - final_handle_rot.as_matrix() @ (rot_handle.as_matrix().T @ delta_pos_handle_to_center)

# Convert final handle rotation back to WXYZ
final_handle_quat_wxyz = np.roll(final_handle_rot.as_quat(), 1)  # XYZW -> WXYZ

# Step 6: Insert the nut by moving to the computed handle pose
goto_pose(final_handle_pos, final_handle_quat_wxyz, z_approach=0.1)
goto_pose(final_handle_pos, final_handle_quat_wxyz)

# Step 7: Release the nut
open_gripper()

# Step 8: Return to home
goto_home_joint_position()
